event: absence of metastases in 24 excised lymph nodes
loss of expression of MLH1 and PMS2
increase in white blood cells and neutrophils
pain in his neck and right leg
increase in creatine kinase (CK) and myoglobin levels
absent reflexes in the biceps, brachioradialis, triceps, and patellar and Achilles tendons
significant stenosis of the right coronary artery
no fibrosis or signs of recent myocardial infarction
50 × 60 mm metastasis and 3-4 up to 5-mm metastases in the right liver lobe
pronounced inflammatory infiltration of lymphocytes, occasional plasma cells and granulocytes, and fibrosis in the intercostal musculature, diaphragm, cervical musculature and tongue
fibrosis in one area of the heart, consistent with myocardial infarction
inflammatory infiltrate in a small area of the heart
microscopical examination of the liver did not show metastases from colorectal cancer but instead a hepatocellular cancer (HCC)
hepatocellular cancer (HCC) positive for hepatocytes and negative for glypican, CDX2, CK20 and CK7
fibrosis stage 2-3 according to Batts and Ludwig in the porta field
cause of death was determined as respiratory insufficiency due to polymyositis
